title: -Update: HR Response: Audit Directive(s) for Comprehensive Domestic Violence Policy to Support City Employees

sections.current_situation:
The audit set forth six recommendations, and implementation of five out of six has been
completed, while the sixth has been partly implemented. Five of the recommendations
were integrated into a finalized domestic violence policy, which is now Administrative
Regulation 2.21 available to all City staff via Sharepoint. The implemented
recommendations relate to revisions of the title of the policy (to reflect a broader scope),
pertinent definitions, factors to be considered in assessing accommodations, supporting
documents required for accommodations, and best practices in implementing such a
policy. The policy was finalized as Administrative Regulation 2.21 effective September
2, 2025.
As to a sixth recommendation regarding staff training for compliance with the policy,
Human Resources has designed a rollout and training plan that provides the A.R. 2.21
to all City staff accompanied by confirmation and a testing of knowledge, understanding,
and affirmation of receipt – all via the NeoGov Learn platform. The materials are
currently being implemented for Citywide staff dissemination.
1 Domestic Violence Response: Berkeley Needs a Comprehensive Policy to Support City Employees:
https://berkeleyca.gov/sites/default/files/2022-01/Domestic-Violence-Response-Report.pdf
Page 2 of 4
UPDATE: HR Response: Audit Directive(s) for Domestic Violence Policy Informational Calendar
October 14, 2025
sections.background:
The City Auditor’s Office issued its report on October 10, 2019. HR’s first informational
update was provided on November 3, 2022, the second on May 14, 2024, and the third
in December 2024. This report is Human Resources’ fourth informational update. The
next update will be Fall 2026.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
There are no identifiable environmental effects or opportunities associated with the
subject of this report.
CONTACT PERSON
Janelle Rodrigues, Director of Human Resources, (510) 981-6807.

sections.preamble:
Page 1 of 4
Office of the City Manager
INFORMATION CALENDAR
October 14, 2025
To: Honorable Mayor and Members of the City Council
From: Paul Buddenhagen, City Manager
Submitted by: Janelle Rodrigues, Director of Human Resources
Subject: UPDATE: HR Response: Audit Directive(s) for Comprehensive Domestic
Violence Policy to Support City Employees
INTRODUCTION
This report is an informational update on progress toward goals recommended by a City
Auditor report titled “Domestic Violence Response: Berkeley Needs a Comprehensive
Policy to Support City Employees” issued on October 10, 2019.1 Specifically, the report
tracks progress toward goals articulated by the Audit’s Corrective Action Plan within the
broader context of a workplace violence prevention policy developed by the Human
Resources Department.
